purpose: Provide a consistent user-facing CLI entrypoint so normal users can start every common workflow with `ananta ...` instead of mixing `ananta init` with `python -m agent.cli_goals ...`. Existing python module invocations may remain as internal/dev fallback but must not be the documented golden path.
scope_rules: Keep this track focused on unified user-facing CLI entrypoints and command UX.
Reuse existing goal/runtime modules instead of duplicating orchestration or policy logic.
Use explicit file:id dependency references only for active todo track files.
Do not couple CLI command wiring to Docker-only runtime assumptions.
non_goals: Do not rewrite the full CLI implementation if existing modules can be wrapped.
Do not duplicate orchestration, policy or approval logic in the CLI.
Do not remove existing `python -m agent.cli_goals` developer entrypoints until compatibility is intentionally deprecated.
Do not make Docker a required dependency for the CLI entrypoint.
success_criteria: A single `ananta` command is available after installation or editable install.

Common workflows are documented as `ananta init`, `ananta status`, `ananta ask`, `ananta plan`, `ananta analyze`, `ananta review`, `ananta diagnose`, `ananta patch`, `ananta repair-admin`, `ananta new-project`, `ananta evolve-project`, `ananta tui`, `ananta doctor` and optionally `ananta web`.
Existing module-based commands remain usable for developers.
README/Quickstart uses only `ananta ...` commands for normal users.
CLI smoke tests prove the user-facing command dispatches to existing behavior without client-side orchestration.

tasks.title: Console script entrypoint add
acceptance_criteria: Project exposes `ananta = agent.cli.main:main` or equivalent console script.
`ananta --help` works after editable install.
Entrypoint performs command dispatch only and does not duplicate Hub orchestration.
Tests verify the console script target can be imported.

tasks.title: Top-level command structure define
acceptance_criteria: Defines stable user-facing subcommands: init, first-run, status, ask, plan, analyze, review, diagnose, patch, repair-admin, new-project, evolve-project, tui, doctor.
Optional web command is included only if a stable backend/UI launcher exists or degrades with a useful message.
Unknown commands show concise help and exit non-zero.
All commands support `--help`.

tasks.title: Init command wire to runtime wizard
acceptance_criteria: `ananta init` dispatches to the runtime profile wizard.
`ananta init --yes --runtime-mode local-dev --llm-backend ollama --model ananta-default` is accepted or clearly rejected with actionable validation.
Init writes reviewable profile/config files and does not require Docker in local-dev mode.
Tests cover non-interactive --yes mode.

tasks.title: Goal command aliases wire to cli_goals
acceptance_criteria: `ananta ask <text>` maps to existing ask/goal behavior.

`ananta plan <text>`, `ananta analyze <text>`, `ananta review <text>`, `ananta diagnose <text>`, `ananta patch <text>`, `ananta repair-admin <text>`, `ananta new-project <text>` and `ananta evolve-project <text>` map to existing cli_goals behavior or return a clear not-yet-supported message.
Mappings preserve existing request payload semantics.
No alias performs direct worker/tool execution locally; it routes through existing Hub/goal paths.

tasks.title: Status first-run doctor commands wire
acceptance_criteria: `ananta first-run` runs or delegates the first-run flow.
`ananta status` shows concise Hub/Worker/LLM/RAG state or a degraded explanation.
`ananta doctor` performs local setup checks and prints actionable next steps.
Commands are safe to run repeatedly.

tasks.title: TUI and optional Web launch commands wire
acceptance_criteria: `ananta tui` launches or delegates to the existing TUI entrypoint.
`ananta web` launches, opens, or prints the configured Web UI URL if supported.
If TUI/Web dependencies are missing, command exits with a helpful degraded message.
No TUI/Web command owns orchestration logic.

tasks.title: Module invocation compatibility keep
acceptance_criteria: Existing `python -m agent.cli_goals ...` developer commands still work or have documented compatibility shims.
Developer docs clearly mark module invocations as internal/dev fallback.
Normal user docs prefer `ananta ...` only.
Tests cover at least one old command path and one new command path.

tasks.title: Quickstart and README command examples update
acceptance_criteria: Quickstart uses `ananta init`, `ananta status`, `ananta ask`, `ananta plan`, `ananta analyze`, `ananta tui` style commands.
No normal-user quickstart command starts with `python -m agent.cli_goals`.
Docs explain local-dev default and optional Docker/Podman modes consistently.
Examples are short and copy-pasteable.

tasks.title: Unified CLI smoke and release gate hook add
acceptance_criteria: Smoke test verifies `ananta --help`, `ananta init --help`, `ananta status --help`, `ananta ask --help` and `ananta tui --help`.
Release gate includes unified CLI smoke when CLI runtime is claimed.
Smoke does not require a live cloud LLM provider.
Failure output tells users which command or packaging entrypoint is broken.

execution_order_summary: Add console script and command dispatcher first.
Wire init, goal aliases and status/doctor commands to existing modules instead of rewriting behavior.
Keep old python -m entrypoints as developer fallback.
Update Quickstart so normal users only see `ananta ...` commands.
Add smoke tests and release-gate coverage.
summary_notes: This track is primarily a UX/DX wrapper track. It should not create another orchestration layer.
The user-facing golden path should start with `ananta init` and continue with `ananta ...` commands everywhere.
Module-based python commands can remain available for developers, tests and backwards compatibility.
This track reuses the existing runtime wizard and goal CLI modules as backend behavior sources.

notes: Stale dependency reference `todo.platform-gaps.json:PG-T01` was replaced with in-track dependency `CLI-T02`.
Scope rules were added to keep this track focused on unified CLI UX without orchestration duplication.
Execution stage rollup data was added for milestone-level progress visibility.
End summaries were added to provide explicit analysis, corrections and extension status.

end_summaries.title: Analyseergebnis
items: Die Track-Struktur war schema-valide und konsistent, enthielt aber einen veralteten Cross-Track-Dependency-Verweis.
Milestone-Rollups lagen nur in `tasks_status_summary` vor, nicht als eigene Stage-Sicht.

end_summaries.title: Korrekturen
items: Abhaengigkeit von CLI-T03 wurde auf aktive In-Track-Tasks umgestellt (`CLI-T01`, `CLI-T02`).
Veraltete Referenz in `summary_notes` auf nicht vorhandene `todo.platform-gaps.json` wurde entfernt.

end_summaries.title: Verbesserungen und Erweiterungen
items: `scope_rules` wurden zur klaren Begrenzung von Track-Intention und Architekturgrenzen ergaenzt.
`analysis_checks` dokumentiert die vorgenommenen Konsistenz- und Strukturverbesserungen.

end_summaries.title: Status am Ende
items: Aktueller Stand: 9 Aufgaben (9 todo, 0 in_progress, 0 blocked, 0 done).
Track ist fuer Umsetzung vorbereitet und enthaelt jetzt explizite End-Summaries.
